definition: Aging and death as the culminating suffering conditioned by birth in the dependent-arising sequence.
notes: The project prefers aging-and-death because it preserves the compound structure and matches the rhythm of the standard sequence. Old age and death is acceptable when a more idiomatic English phrase is wanted in explanatory prose.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where a more familiar English phrase helps
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate outside tightly patterned formula translation.

example_phrases.notes: Closing link of the standard sequence.

example_phrases.translation: sorrow, lamentation, dissatisfaction, distress, and despair come to be
example_phrases.notes: Standard expansion following aging-and-death in the formula.
